info.name: SaaS Express Boilerplate API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Enterprise SaaS Express.js + MongoDB Boilerplate.

## Setup
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to your server (default: `http://localhost:3000`)
2. Register a user → Login → the `accessToken` variable is auto-set
3. All protected routes automatically use the Bearer token

## Variables
- `baseUrl` – API base URL
- `accessToken` – JWT access token (auto-set on login)
- `refreshToken` – JWT refresh token (auto-set on login)
- `userId` – Current user ID (auto-set on login)
- `orgId` – Organization ID (auto-set on login)

item.name: 🔐 Authentication
item.description: All authentication endpoints — register, login, token management, password reset, email verification, and 2FA.

request.description:
Register a new user. Optionally provide `organizationName` to create an organization simultaneously.

**Validation rules:**
- `firstName`, `lastName`: 1–50 chars
- `email`: valid email
- `password`: min 8 chars, must contain uppercase, lowercase, and a digit
- `organizationName`: optional, 2–100 chars

exec: console.log('⚠️ 2FA required — use the Verify 2FA request');

request.description:
Login with email and password. Returns an `accessToken` (15 min TTL) and sets an `httpOnly` `refreshToken` cookie (30 days).

If 2FA is enabled on the account, returns `requiresTwoFactor: true` and a `twoFactorToken` challenge — use the **Verify 2FA** request to complete login.

Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token. The refresh token is typically sent via httpOnly cookie automatically, but can also be passed in the request body.

Refresh tokens are rotated on each use (old one is invalidated, new one issued).

Get the currently authenticated user's profile. Requires a valid Bearer token.

Returns the full user object excluding sensitive fields (password, tokens, 2FA secret).

Logout the current session. Blacklists the access token and invalidates the refresh token.

The refresh token cookie is also cleared.

Invalidate ALL refresh tokens for the current user, effectively logging out from every device/session.

The current access token (15 min TTL) may still work until it expires naturally.

Request a password reset email. Always returns success to prevent email enumeration — even if the email doesn't exist in the system.

The reset link is valid for **10 minutes**.

**Rate limited:** 3 requests per hour per IP.

Reset password using the token received via email. The token is a one-time-use token valid for 10 minutes.

Successful reset also invalidates ALL existing refresh tokens (logs out all devices).

Change password for the currently authenticated user. Requires knowledge of the current password.

Successful change invalidates ALL existing sessions (refresh tokens).

Verify a user's email address using the token sent in the verification email.

Token is valid for **24 hours**. On success, user status changes from `pending_verification` to `active`.

Resend the email verification link to the currently authenticated user's email address.

Useful when the original verification email expired or was lost.

Complete a login that requires 2FA. Submit the `challengeToken` from the login response along with the current 6-digit TOTP code from the authenticator app.

The challenge token expires in **5 minutes**.

item.name: 👤 Users
item.description: User profile management — view, update, delete your own profile, manage sessions, update preferences, upload avatar. Admin endpoints for user management.

request.description:
Get the full profile of the currently authenticated user, including populated organization details.

Excludes: password, tokens, 2FA secret, refresh tokens.

Update the current user's profile.

**Updatable fields:** `firstName`, `lastName`, `phone`, `username`, `preferences`

Fields not in this list are ignored. To change password, use the Change Password endpoint.

Soft-delete the current user's account. Sets `deletedAt` timestamp and status to `inactive`.

⚠️ This action cannot be undone via the API. The account data is retained for audit purposes.

List all active sessions (refresh tokens) for the current user.

Shows: device, IP address, user agent, created date, expiry for each session.

Update user preferences.

**Options:**
- `language`: language code (e.g. `en`, `fr`, `de`)
- `timezone`: IANA timezone (e.g. `America/New_York`, `UTC`)
- `theme`: `light`, `dark`, or `system`
- `notifications.email`: boolean
- `notifications.push`: boolean
- `notifications.sms`: boolean

formdata.description: Image file (jpg, png, gif, webp). Max 10MB.

request.description:
Upload a profile avatar image. Accepts: `jpg`, `jpeg`, `png`, `gif`, `webp`. Max size: 10MB.

The image is automatically processed (resized to 400×400 original, 80×80 thumbnail) and uploaded to AWS S3.

Returns both `original` and `thumbnail` URLs.

query.description: Sort field. Prefix with - for descending (e.g. -createdAt, name)
Filter by status: active, inactive, suspended, pending_verification
Filter by role: user, admin, super_admin, viewer
Search by firstName, lastName, or email

request.description:
**Requires:** `admin` or `super_admin` role.

List all users with pagination, filtering, sorting, and field selection.

**Filters:** `status`, `role`, `organizationId`, `search`
**Sort:** Use `-` prefix for descending (e.g. `-createdAt`)
**Fields:** Comma-separated field names for sparse responses

**Requires:** `admin` or `super_admin` role.

Get a specific user by MongoDB ObjectId. Includes populated organization info.

**Requires:** `admin` or `super_admin` role.

Update any user's profile fields.

**Requires:** `super_admin` role.

Soft-delete a user account by ID. Sets `deletedAt` timestamp and changes status to `inactive`.

The user can no longer log in but data is preserved.

item.name: 🏢 Organizations
item.description: Organization (tenant) management — list your organizations, view organization details.

request.description:
Get all organizations the current user is a member of.

Returns full organization objects including billing info, plan details, member list, and settings.

Get a specific organization by ID.

**Access control:** User must be a member of the organization, or a `super_admin`.

Returns full organization details including current plan, usage stats, billing info, and member list.

item.name: 💳 Billing
item.description: Stripe billing — invoices, customer portal. Requires Stripe to be configured.

request.description:
Get the last 10 invoices for the current user's organization.

**Requires:** Stripe configured with `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`

Returns: invoice ID, number, status, amount, currency, date, PDF download URL.

Generate a Stripe Customer Portal URL for the current user's organization.

The portal lets users:
- View and download invoices
- Update payment methods
- Cancel or upgrade subscriptions

**Requires:** Stripe configured with an active customer ID.

item.name: 🔧 Admin
item.description: Super admin only endpoints — system statistics, queue monitoring.

request.description:
**Requires:** `super_admin` role.

Get system-level statistics:
- **Queue stats:** waiting, active, completed, failed, delayed jobs per queue
- **Database status:** connection state, host, pool size

Useful for monitoring dashboards and health checks.

header.description: Stripe webhook signature (set automatically by Stripe)

request.description:
Stripe webhook receiver. This endpoint is called by Stripe — not by your frontend.

**Supported events:**
- `customer.subscription.created`
- `customer.subscription.updated`
- `customer.subscription.deleted`
- `invoice.payment_succeeded`
- `invoice.payment_failed`

⚠️ The `stripe-signature` header is verified using `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Testing manually will fail signature verification unless you disable it or use the Stripe CLI (`stripe listen`).

item.name: 🏥 Health & Docs
item.description: Health check and API documentation endpoints. No authentication required.

request.description:
System health check endpoint.

Checks:
- **Database:** MongoDB connection state and ping latency
- **Redis:** Connection status
- **Memory:** Total, used, free, and process heap

Returns `200` when healthy, `503` when degraded.

Does **not** require authentication.

Open the interactive Swagger UI documentation in a browser.

Provides a full OpenAPI 3.0 spec with request/response schemas for all endpoints.
